title: senecio ionophyllus var. stygius
intro: Senecio ionophyllus var. stygius, also known as the Stygian groundsel, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern parts of the Western Cape province in South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its bioactive compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Stygian groundsel include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and derive its health benefits.
